column1: # Edit `selected` to `yes` for fixes you want applied.

column7: finding
`touch_last_used` keyed by `(name, user_id)` is still semantically unsafe.
Subscription support is only planned for `SkillService::list()`, not the full skill read path.
Variables query uses `UNION ALL` without dedupe guarantees.
`list_user_ids()` predicate `user_id != ''` is incomplete.
Revocation/expiry semantics for subscriptions are not included in query filters.
Sync-loop cleanup behavior is missing for unsubscribed/revoked resources.
No explicit conflict policy for same skill name across owned and subscribed sets.
Testing scope is underspecified for cross-tenant regressions.
Authorization drift risk between DB truth and filesystem mirror is not addressed.
Ignoring path `agent_id` entirely creates API contract ambiguity and audit risk.
N+1/per-user subscription sync can become expensive and inconsistent under load.
Prefer a single “accessible resources” query abstraction per resource type.
Consider replacing path `agent_id` routes with user-scoped routes over time.
Missing explicit subscription-state checks is a direct authorization concern.
